salah_guide.title: States and Positions of Salah (Namaz)
salah_guide.description: A structured list of the physical and verbal states of the Islamic prayer.

states.action: Raising hands to the ears and saying 'Allahu Akbar' to begin the prayer.
Standing with hands folded, reciting Surah Al-Fatihah and additional verses from the Quran.
Leaning forward with hands on knees, keeping the back straight.
Returning to a standing position with arms at the sides.
Placing the forehead, nose, palms, knees, and toes on the ground in total submission.
Sitting upright on the legs briefly between the two prostrations.
Sitting at the end of the second or final unit to recite the testimony of faith.
Turning the head to the right and then the left, saying 'Assalamu alaikum wa rahmatullah'.
